body: WARRANT#82 shipped -> SLS(87)/10.01, a real one this time: g_ForgeChampion/g_ForgeChampionSet (the ratchet's entire memory of the current best scorecard) had NO save/load site anywhere in the codebase -- confirmed by grep. Every restart silently forgot the champion, so ForgeRatchetPromote's own 'no champion yet, first scorecard becomes champion by definition' branch fired every cold start, which defeats a monotonic ratchet's whole purpose across restarts: a worse post-restart challenger could freely become champion with nothing to be non-inferior to. Fixed with guarded-tail persistence (same pattern as the ProbDB and SR-history tails), placed as the new last tail so a schema mismatch fails safe. Purely additive, fails open on older files exactly like the prior (broken) behavior -- can only improve, never regress. Compiled 0 errors, diff-verified against SLS(86), deployed to the terminal's real Experts folder, ledger at pass_67. This is the third warrant this session (WARRANT#80 bracket-fallback, WARRANT#81 threshold-to-target diagnostic, WARRANT#82 champion persistence) -- all gated or purely diagnostic, all unvalidated against a real run since none has happened since SLS(68)'s STAND DOWN. Real floor for this session: everything left genuinely needs either the Terminal-closing mystery resolved so a live run can happen, or a substantially larger dedicated build (r8's reversion-trigger design, forge-edge-2's full Convergence Sigma assembly) rather than another bounded warrant. Source, ex4, ledger attached.
